source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Any linear dc network (a load $R$ is connected between the two arbitrary points A and B of the network) is equivalent to a series circuit consisting of a battery of emf $V$ and a resistance $r$, as shown in Fig. 3.4. Calculate $V$ and $r$ of the circuit in Fig. 3.7. (Hint: Use mathematical induction)

metadata.reasoning_chain: By induction we have $V = \frac{1}{2}\{V_{1} + \frac{1}{2}[V_{2} + \frac{1}{2}(V_{3} + \cdots + \frac{1}{2}(V_{n-1} + \frac{1}{2}V_{n} \underbrace{) \cdots}_{n-1})]\} = 2^{-1}V_{1} + 2^{-2}V_{2} + \cdots + 2^{-n}V_{n}$, and $r = R$
